prompt: A striking, single-frame photomontage poster designed in the strict grammar of Soviet Constructivism, 1920s. The composition is built on a radical diagonal axis. The central focus is a stylized, bold technical spotlight or industrial lens, composed of angular red and black shapes. From this lens, a focused, intense beam of red and white light projects onto a singular, massive word in Russian constructivist sans-serif typography: "ВНИМАНИЕ" (ATTENTION). This word is large, black, and powerful, dominating the upper right. Below and behind it, other smaller Russian words are scattered, fading and smaller (representing unfocused data): "ДАННЫЕ" (DATA), "ШУМ" (NOISE), "ПОТОК" (STREAM). These elements are rendered in a photomontage style, integrating industrial textures and textures of printed ink on coarse paper. The palette is strictly red, black, and cream/white. Strong negative space, hard-edged lines, and dynamic movement. High resolution, textured print effect.
tradition_approach: I used dynamic diagonal compositions, a limited red-black-white palette, and integrated bold, angular sans-serif typography as a structural element (not a label), which is a core visual grammatical rule of Rodchenko and Lissitzky.
